records.ety: [OE. lispen, lipsen, AS. wlisp stammering, lisping; akin to D. & OHG. lispen to lisp, G. lispeln, Sw. läspa, Dan. lespe.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Lisped (lspt); p. pr. & vb. n. Lisping.]

defs: To pronounce the sibilant letter s imperfectly; to give s and z the sound of th; -- a defect common among children.
To speak with imperfect articulation; to mispronounce, as a child learning to talk.
quotes: As yet a child, nor yet a fool to fame, I lisped in numbers, for the numbers came.

defs: To speak hesitatingly with a low voice, as if afraid.
quotes: Lest when my lisping, guilty tongue should halt.

defs: To utter with imperfect articulation; to express with words pronounced imperfectly or indistinctly, as a child speaks; hence, to express by the use of simple, childlike language.
quotes: To speak unto them after their own capacity, and to lisp the words unto them according as the babes and children of that age might sound them again.

defs: To speak with reserve or concealment; to utter timidly or confidentially; as, to lisp treason.
The habit or act of lisping. See Lisp, v. i., 1.
quotes: I overheard her answer, with a very pretty lisp, “O! Strephon, you are a dangerous creature.”

defs: a high-level computer programming language in which statements and data are in the form of lists, enclosed in parentheses; -- used especially for rapid development of prototype programs in artificial intelligence applications .
